tf.linalg.cholesky_solve requires two inputs: 'chol' (Cholesky factor L) and 'rhs' (right-hand side tensor), whereas torch.cholesky_inverse only takes L and computes inv(L @ L.T). The original TF test case is missing the mandatory 'rhs' argument. To make it semantically equivalent, we must provide rhs = I (identity matrix) so that cholesky_solve(chol, rhs) returns inv(L @ L.T), matching torch.cholesky_inverse's behavior. Also, TF expects chol to be lower-triangular (default in tf.linalg.cholesky), and torch.cholesky_inverse defaults to lower=False (i.e., upper triangular), so we must align by setting upper=False in PyTorch or generate L as lower-triangular consistently. Since input is only shape/dtype, we repair by generating a valid positive-definite matrix A, computing its Cholesky factor L (lower), then using L as chol and identity as rhs in TF; for PyTorch, we pass L with upper=False.
